title: Unit tests: pad() extended bucket + FileHeader/FileChunk roundtrip
description:
## What
Tests for the extended pad() bucket and new ClearMessage variants. Already partially described in nie-pf86.1 but extracted here for clarity.

## Algorithm
These tests live in core/src/messages.rs:
1. pad_accepts_65532_bytes: let data = vec![0xabu8; 65532]; let out = pad(&data).unwrap(); assert_eq!(out.len(), 65536); let back = unpad(&out).unwrap(); assert_eq!(back, data);
2. pad_rejects_65533_bytes: let data = vec![0u8; 65533]; assert!(pad(&data).is_err());
3. file_header_roundtrips: create FileHeader with all fields, serialize to JSON, deserialize, assert all fields equal.
4. file_chunk_data_is_base64_in_json: create FileChunk with data = vec![0xffu8; 16], serialize, parse as serde_json::Value, assert value['data'] is a string (not array), assert B64.decode(the_string) == original data (using base64 engine). Oracle: RFC 4648 base64 is the encoding standard - ///// is 5 bytes of 0xFF... check exact value.
5. file_chunk_roundtrips_48kb: create FileChunk with data = vec created by repeating [0..=255] enough times to fill 48*1024 bytes, roundtrip, assert data identical.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-core passes; all 5 tests pass
close_reason: All 5 tests (pad_accepts_65532_bytes, pad_rejects_65533_bytes, file_header_roundtrips, file_chunk_data_is_base64_in_json, file_chunk_roundtrips_binary_data) were implemented as part of nie-pf86.1
